scores.rationale: Most honest proposal about the ceiling: it says outright that V1/V2 cannot beat PTGui and only the V3 flow bolt-on can — but that V3 discards the entire ffmpeg maskedmerge-cascade blend graph (the thing V1/V2 spent ~8 days building) for a cv2 pipe loop that is essentially Proposal B. The nearest-only remap limitation (verified on this machine) forces the 2x-supersample-then-lanczos workaround, which is why runtime is worst-in-class (0.8–2 fps, 2.5–5.5 h/catalog at SS=2) and iteration is slow. Maintenance fit is excellent for a TS/ffmpeg shop while it stays ffmpeg, but the path to the actual quality goal exits ffmpeg anyway. Calibration story is strong (mercy01.jpg SSIM harness gates every prep, zero-distortion degraded mode, per-roll sky re-solve ladder). Verified capabilities on-machine (remap options, encoders) show real grounding, though its integration paths cite a nonexistent pipeline root (real one is meridian_public_site/pipeline/src). Net: a safe, auditable MVP whose core blend architecture is throwaway relative to the stated beat-PTGui goal.

The only proposal where the MVP engine is the same engine that carries to the beat-PTGui claim: float32 cv2.remap, frozen graph-cut seams, and multiband from day one, then bidirectional DIS + forward-backward gating + edge-aware fill + alpha-ramp novel-view morph layered on top — the most complete parallax recipe of the three, including the honest PTGui-batch-render baseline for the A/B claim. Strongest calibration rigor: M0 hard gate reprojecting the 103 control points (<1.5 px RMS) plus mercy01.jpg diff before any video, and it is the only proposal that explicitly flags the calibrate.py sensor-diagonal bug (22.56 vs 30.56 mm). All cited paths verified correct on disk, and the PyInstaller single-binary precedent is real (build.sh:62). Weaknesses: Python runtime blowout is the acknowledged big risk (0.2–0.5 s/frame budget is optimistic with 50–150 ms/frame Python overhead already admitted); one dependency slip — ximgproc.fastGlobalSmootherFilter requires opencv-contrib, not opencv-python-headless as pinned; and its 100 GB free-disk precheck will fail today (47 GiB free), which at least means the precheck works.

Best performance by an order of magnitude (40–80 fps MVP, catalog in 5–15 min; V3 flow at 4–10 fps) and fastest to a visible win, because the warp/blend already exists in-repo (RemapCompute.metal, StitchShaders.metal, RemapGenerator.swift all verified present). The containment strategy for the Swift/Metal maintenance problem (frozen semver CLI contract, prebuilt SHA-pinned binary, golden-frame CI, documented Python escape hatch) is the most thoughtful treatment of that risk in any proposal — but it does not make the risk go away for a one-person TypeScript shop: headless-ifying display-bound MTKView/CVDisplayLink code, owning AVAssetWriter, Vision-API drift across macOS updates, and effectively maintaining two implementations (Metal + the Python fallback). MVP ships with distortion zeroed (honestly quantified at ~3–4 px on proxies) with the convention fix deferred to V2, slightly weaker than A/B's validate-first stance. Its stage placement between discover and probe is the most invasive pipeline reordering, and like A it cites a nonexistent pipeline root path. High ceiling, high speed, highest structural risk.

synthesis:
B wins because it is the only architecture with no throwaway rung: A's ffmpeg maskedmerge cascade gets discarded the moment the flow engine (the only thing that beats PTGui) arrives, and C's Metal excellence is structurally mismatched to a one-person TS/ffmpeg shop no matter how well contained. B's MVP static path (float remap + graph-cut seams + multiband) is the identical code path the flow morph later plugs into, its calibration validation is the most rigorous (103-CP reprojection gate before any video), and it was the only proposal whose file paths all resolved correctly on the actual machine.

Graft from A: (1) make the mercy01.jpg SSIM validation a standing gate that runs at every prep, not a one-time M0 spike — calibration conventions can silently regress when the per-roll sky re-solve merges new angles; (2) A's degraded-mode ladder verbatim — never block publish, ring-band-only master with sky black + degraded flag beats a scrambled zenith (Roll02_Clip020 lesson), zero-distortion mode as the convention-failure fallback (Zero-D lenses make it ~2–4 px); (3) --dry-run printing the full auditable command line, and recording tool version + full argv hash in every manifest; (4) the per-clip seam-yaw rotation rescue for a stubborn near-field object — cheap manual knob, high save value; (5) the 50-frame nona/multiblend excerpt as a static-quality ceiling reference, run once as a decision gate.

Graft from C: (1) C's headless-ification table is the concrete plan for B's own listed Metal escape hatch — treat it as pre-de-risked and trigger it if the Clip04 MVP throughput checkpoint misses 0.5 s/frame; (2) golden-frame CI (render frames 10/100/500 of Clip04, compare to stored references) — B has determinism claims but no regression harness to enforce them; (3) key idempotency on input hashes + args + tool version, NOT output bytes — B's byte-identical-chunk claim is fragile across ffmpeg/OS updates and C is right about this; (4) C's exit-code taxonomy with report-always-written-on-failure including failedStage; (5) C's quantified distortion-zeroed error budget (~3–4 px at proxy res) as the documented degraded mode. Also fix B's dependency pin: ximgproc lives in opencv-contrib-python-headless, or drop the fast-global-smoother for a guided-filter hole fill from the main module.

Adversarial notes beyond the missed_by_all list: B's 12-frame TC spread tolerance and C's --allow-untimed both paper over the real question of WHY spread would exceed the Roll01 pattern — that signature usually means a camera restarted mid-roll and its frames are unusable, not offsettable. And all three treat the 7-clip catalog as the workload; the moment this works, the catalog grows, which is when C's performance argument returns — hence keeping the Metal escape hatch warm rather than aspirational.
missed_by_all: Disk space is already broken today: the studio Mac has 47 GiB free (df verified, 90% full). Every proposal's plan — copy 9-cam sources from SMB to local scratch (~15+ GB), write 35–55 GB of ProRes masters locally, plus A's hugin bake-off 'TBs of scratch' — cannot fit. Masters must stream to the NAS or use per-clip scratch-then-evict, and B's 100 GB precheck would refuse to run at all. Nobody actually checked the disk.

Gamma/linear color trap: the proxies are gamma-encoded bt709 (verified yuv422p10le/bt709). All three apply 2^EV exposure gains and vignette division directly on encoded RGB, but PTGui's exposureoffset and vignetting model are defined in linear light through the camera response curve solved in the .pts — which none of the three parse. Gains multiplied in gamma space under-correct by ~1/2.4 power; the least-squares gain solves will converge to biased values and subtle seam brightness steps will survive all the geometry work.

Range/matrix pinning at every rawvideo pipe boundary: B and A-V3 pipe frames between ffmpeg and cv2 as rgb48/rawvideo, but no proposal pins -color_range (ProRes is limited range), the 709 matrix, or chroma siting on those conversions. A silent limited-vs-full mismatch crushes or lifts blacks in the master AND corrupts every SSIM/flicker metric the QA harness produces, making the A/B evidence worthless while looking plausible.

Sub-frame sync and rolling shutter: TC alignment is frame-quantized. Without genlock the nine cameras' exposure instants differ by up to ~±20 ms (half a frame at 24fps) and can drift within a clip; moving subjects then show temporal parallax at seams that static stitching cannot fix and that optical flow will misattribute to geometry (breathing seams on motion). None propose mid-clip sync verification (e.g., audio cross-correlation across cams) or mention rolling-shutter skew, which differs per camera pointing direction.

Watermark/grade ordering in the upgraded rendition: all three bump the preview 960→2880 and reuse watermarkFilter + PREVIEW_GRADE, but that chain was authored for the fake hconcat pano — burn-in position/scale and the grade need re-derivation for a band crop, QA metrics and A/B reels must be computed pre-grade and pre-watermark, and the hconcat fallback must pass through the identical grade+watermark chain or on-site before/after comparisons (and the fallback-flagging story) are not like-for-like.

No spherical metadata: none of the three inject equirect/spherical V2 metadata (sv3d/st3d boxes or the spatial-media XML) into the 3840x1920 masters or previews, so no downstream player, buyer QC tool, or stock marketplace will recognize the '$8k/min pro' deliverable as 360 content — it will present as a distorted flat video. Related: only A even mentions the black nadir hole, and nobody specifies a nadir patch or deliverable spec for what a paying buyer receives.
mvp_slice: B's M0 + a single-clip vertical slice, in this order: (1) parse mercy01.pts v33 in Python and validate the warp against ground truth already on disk — reproject the 103 control points (<1.5 px RMS gate) and SSIM-diff a rendered still against mercy01.jpg — before touching any video; (2) then for Roll01_Clip04 only: TC-trim (known offsets A1 B3 C2 D2 E2 F1, 1631 frames), bake cached float32 LUTs, per-clip gain lock, frozen graph-cut seams with plain feather (defer multiband and all flow), composite the ring band into a 3840x1920 ProRes master written to the NAS (not local — 47 GiB free), and wire stitch.ts → drop.stitchedMaster → the 2880-wide watermarked preview with hconcat as the audited fallback. One clip live on the site end-to-end (~5–6 days) proves the calibration conventions, the TC contract, the pipeline integration, the disk/SMB reality, and visibly beats the xstack fake — before any generalization to 7 clips, sky tier, or flow. The control-point validation gate is the single highest-leverage piece of work in any of the three proposals: every downstream day is wasted if the PTGui convention is wrong, and both prior failures in this codebase (the zeroed Swift distortion, the wrong tan-space radius) were exactly this mistake.
